title: Implement URL Shortener REST API in Go with In-Memory Storage

steps.description: Define URL structure and core business logic for URL shortening and retrieval.
steps.done_condition: Domain logic successfully encapsulated in defined value objects and entities.

steps.description: Create interfaces for URL management to be exposed by the API.
steps.done_condition: Ports for URL management are defined and ready for integration.

steps.description: Implement HTTP adapters to handle incoming REST requests and map them to domain functionality.
steps.done_condition: HTTP adapters are successfully processing requests and interfacing with the domain logic.

steps.description: Run tests to verify that adapters are correctly interacting with the domain and ports.

mergeOrder: Merge domain and ports first, then integrate primary adapters.

riskRegister.mitigation: Consider implementing persistent storage in future iterations.
riskRegister.risk: Data loss due to in-memory storage.

successCriteria: The URL shortener API is fully functional with in-memory storage and responsive to user requests.
